Strip_Summary: You built the smartest thing on Earth.
On Friday, they fire you from it.
By Tuesday  -  you own it again.
Young stickman alone while peers gather in school hallway. Not fitting in anywhere.
POV: child hands on keyboard, blinking cursor on dark screen. Age 8, first computer.
Child stickman receiving first computer. Curious expression, reaching for laptop.
Child stickman taking apart computer on desk. Parts scattered. Curious intense focus.
Child stickman at keyboard, code on screen. Systems make more sense than people.
Teen stickman in chat room on screen. Other avatars visible. First belonging.
Teen stickman typing fast, screen glowing. No longer slowing down.
Teen stickman reading AI textbook at desk. Age 14, laptop and book open side by side.
Two panels: left age 14 reading about AI, right age 19 dropping out of university.
University hallway. TALL stickman walking out past classmates. Bored expression.
Stickman sketching app wireframe on laptop. Small idea, location app concept.
Split: left, stickman holding phone showing app; right, number 43M on screen. Age 26.
Stickman looking at check/screen showing . Not enough for what he really wants.
Stickman staring at horizon. Something bigger than an app. Something that thinks.
VERY TALL stickman standing at podium before room of seated founders. Age 28, in charge of the launchpad.
VERY TALL stickman at podium. Youngest leader. Room full of founders.
Rows of faceless founder stickmen pitching, building. Billions in future value.
None of them are building what you want.
VERY TALL stickman standing alone, gazing at empty whiteboard. The thing that thinks.
Stickman leaving accelerator building. Starting new lab. Research nonprofit.
Stickman at press conference, microphone. Telling world: building this to give it away.
Humanity. The betterment of all humanity.
You just never define which one.
Top researchers leaving corporate offices. Walking toward the lab. Million-dollar salaries abandoned.
Researchers approaching VERY TALL stickman. Coming to him. Believing.
Because they believe you.
Growing team around whiteboard. Small then bigger. Lab expanding.
Lab at night. Whiteboards full of equations. Alignment arguments. Intense atmosphere.
Pizza boxes at 2AM in lab. Cold slices. Nobody noticing.
Balding stickman with rectangular glasses and beard, the conscience, working late in lab.
Chief scientist (glasses, beard, balding) staring at screen. Believes more than anyone.
Remember that.
VERY TALL stickman at keynote stage with microphone. Promising safety in every speech.
Split screen: model benchmarks climbing. Faster. Smarter. Every week.
Large corporate buildings looming. Bigger companies paying attention. More money, more data.
But speed is what gets funded.
Handshake with faceless suited investor figure. Billion-dollar deal. Then ten billion more.
Nonprofit sign slowly morphing. Something else forming underneath. Not calling it that.
Original team members walking away from lab. Quietly. No scene.
They just stop believing you.
November. Finger pressing launch button on laptop. Chatbot released.
Massive crowd of faceless stickmen holding glowing phones. Chatbot explosion, 100M users.
Montage: faceless stickmen using chatbot on phones/laptops. Writing, planning, talking to it.
VERY TALL stickman scrolling phone numbers. Cold coffee on desk. Not noticing.
Globe montage: news headlines, world waking up. Everyone talking, afraid, wanting it.
Congressional hearing room. VERY TALL stickman at witness table. Magazine covers background.
Senators (faceless) listening. VERY TALL stickman explaining from witness chair.
Senators nodding. Not understanding. VERY TALL stickman explaining simpler, slower.
VERY TALL stickman walking out of hearing room. Thinks he understands now.
Dark interior of lab. Tension building. Something shifting that can't be named.
Chief scientist (balding, glasses, beard) confronting VERY TALL stickman. Asking about direction.
Tense meeting. Questions about investors, direction, speed. Atmosphere heavy.
Lab boardroom. Nobody asking about safety anymore. Different question now.
VERY TALL stickman tapping table edge once. Nervous fidget. Then stopping.
Closeup: VERY TALL stickman shoulders raised, tense posture. Dread wash background.
Friday. VERY TALL stickman receiving phone message. Normal morning.
One line. Meet me.
VERY TALL stickman walking to meeting room. No concern yet.
Video call screen: four faceless figures. Rehearsed words. We don't trust you.
They don't say why.
VERY TALL stickman asking why. Same sentence repeated. Different words.
Chief scientist (glasses, beard) on screen looking at camera. Not at him. Betrayal.
VERY TALL stickman walking away from glass building. Backpack on shoulder, phone in hand. Cold blue.
VERY TALL figure outside building. Cold air. Night atmosphere. Noticing the cold.
Door closing behind. VERY TALL stickman walking away. Six hours ago ran the company.
VERY TALL stickman standing still. Built this. Named it. Hired everyone inside.
Back of car. VERY TALL stickman silent. No destination given to driver.
POV: glowing phone screen in dark car interior. Brightness hurting. Adult hands holding phone.
Apartment interior. Saturday. Phone buzzing non-stop on table.
Phone screen flooding with messages. People from years ago. People barely known.
Split: co-founder on phone call. Both removed from board. Unsure.
Sunday. News headline on screen: new CEO announced. Someone never worked with.
700 stickmen signing letter. Almost entire workforce choosing him.
Close on letter being signed. Choosing SUBJECT over the board. Loyalty moment.
He says he regrets it.
48 hours counter. No company, no title, no desk. But something more dangerous.
VERY TALL stickman alone. No company but has loyalty. Dangerous possession.
Competitor CEO (faceless suited figure) offering team, budget. Fresh start pitch.
VERY TALL stickman considering. Start over. No board. No politics. Almost makes sense.
VERY TALL stickman saying no. Not about building new. About taking back what is his.
VERY TALL stickman walking back through building door. Same hallway. Chest skip moment.
VERY TALL stickman entering hallway. People clapping, crying on both sides.
VERY TALL stickman walking through crowd. Just walking. No reaction to applause.
This time, no one can make you leave.
VERY TALL stickman at boardroom. Old board gone. Chief scientist gone too.
Empty desk where chief scientist used to sit. No goodbye. Just stopped showing up.
VERY TALL stickman seated at head of boardroom table. New chairs. Power position.
Different chairs. Your chairs.
Two panels: left, company growing with stickmen at desks; right, valuation numbers on screen.
Stock chart climbing exponentially. Valuation doubling, doubling again.
Financial reports on screen. Billions in losses every quarter. Not thinking about it.
Signing papers at desk. Converting nonprofit to for-profit. Pen on document.
Press conference. Explaining the conversion. What everyone warned about happening.
The exact thing you said would never happen.
VERY TALL stickman alone at top of corporate pyramid. Controls what no one should control.
And now there's no one left to stop you.
Mirror reflection. Person in mirror stopped asking questions a while ago.
Courtroom. Co-founder (faceless) at plaintiff table. Suing for fraud.
Headlines montage: lied, trick, nonprofit was a scheme. Comparison to fraudsters.
Magazine covers. Courtroom sketches. Lawyers. Reporters outside office building.
VERY TALL stickman trying to explain to someone. Words not coming out right.
You stop trying after that.
Stack of investigative reports on desk. Timelines. Leaked emails printed out.
Report pages highlighted: moved too fast, knew risks, shipped anyway.
Report highlighted: told board one thing, did another.
VERY TALL stickman reading reports alone. Doesnt agree with most. Some he doesnt argue.
VERY TALL stickman staring at screen. Question: saving humanity or selling it.
Screen with numbers. Everything looks fine. Not liking thinking about this.
Something wrong in the numbers. Not wrong yet. That's what bothers him.
VERY TALL stickman standing alone in server room. Machines floor-to-ceiling. Hum of processors. Blue glow.
VERY TALL stickman in server room. Something blurs behind vision. Blinks. Clears.
VERY TALL stickman leaving server room. Lights stay on. They never turn off.
VERY TALL stickman back in dark room. Laptop open. Cursor blinking. Same glow as age 8.
VERY TALL stickman alone with laptop. Built the smartest thing. It got him fired.
You took it back.
Dark background fading. One question remaining. No one can answer.
Did you take control of it, or did it take control of you?
